title: elaeocarpus tuasivicus
intro: Elaeocarpus tuasivicus, also known as the "Niu Manu" or "Kiwai Island Olive", is a species of flowering plant in the family Elaeocarpaceae. Native to the Pacific Islands, particularly in Papua New Guinea, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The leaves and roots of Elaeocarpus tuasivicus are used to make herbal preparations, such as teas and infusions, which are believed to have health benefits including reducing fever, alleviating pain and swelling, and supporting digestive health. Additionally, the plant's extracts have been studied for their potential antioxidant and antimicrobial properties, further highlighting its potential uses in traditional medicine.
